sample_docs.title: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi
sample_docs.summary: PM-KISAN is a 100% centrally funded scheme that provides income support of Rs. 6,000 per year to all landholding farmer families across India. The amount is disbursed in three equal instalments of Rs. 2,000 every four months directly into Aadhaar-linked bank accounts via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT...<truncated>
sample_docs.objective: To supplement the financial needs of farmers in procuring agricultural inputs and meeting domestic needs, ensuring proper crop health and appropriate yield.
sample_docs.office_addresses: Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare, Krishi Bhawan, New Delhi - 110001
sample_docs.how_to_apply: Step 1: Visit the official PM-KISAN portal Go to the homepage of pmkisan.gov.in (https://pmkisan.gov.in) -> Step 2: Click on 'New Farmer Registration' under Farmers Corner Choose 'Rural Farmer Registration' or 'Urban Farmer Registration' (https://pmkisan.gov.in/RegistrationFormupdated.aspx) -> Step ...<truncated>
application_process: Step 1: Visit the official PM-KISAN portal Go to the homepage of pmkisan.gov.in (https://pmkisan.gov.in)
Step 2: Click on 'New Farmer Registration' under Farmers Corner Choose 'Rural Farmer Registration' or 'Urban Farmer Registration' (https://pmkisan.gov.in/RegistrationFormupdated.aspx)
Step 3: Enter Aadhaar number, select state and captcha System checks if farmer is already registered
Step 4: Fill the registration form Enter personal details, bank account details (account number, IFSC), land details
Step 5: Upload required documents Upload Aadhaar, land records, bank passbook
eligibility: Must be a landholding farmer family (husband, wife and minor children)
Bank account must be linked with Aadhaar
Land records must be updated in state revenue records
conditions: Farmer families holding constitutional posts excluded
Serving/retired officers of Central/State Government excluded (except Group D employees)
All serving or retired employees of PSUs and autonomous bodies of Central/State Government excluded
Professionals registered under Professional bodies like doctors, engineers, lawyers, CAs etc. excluded
required_documents: Bank account passbook with IFSC code
benefits: 000 per instalment every 4 months) directly deposited into Aadhaar-linked bank accounts via DBT. Reduces dependency on moneylenders.
sample_docs.coverage: Pan India - All States and Union Territories
sample_docs.target_beneficiaries: All landholding farmer families with cultivable land
sample_docs.financial_assistance_amount: Rs. 6,000 per year (3 instalments of Rs. 2,000 each)

sample_docs.title: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Maandhan Yojana
sample_docs.summary: PM-KMY is a voluntary and contributory pension scheme launched on September 12, 2019, providing old-age social security to small and marginal farmers. Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC) is the Pension Fund Manager. Farmers contribute between Rs. 55 to Rs. 200 per month (matched equally by the...<truncated>
sample_docs.objective: To provide social security in the form of minimum pension of Rs. 3,000 per month to small and marginal farmers at the age of 60 years.
sample_docs.office_addresses: LIC of India, Central Office, Yogakshema, Jeevan Bima Marg, Mumbai - 400021
sample_docs.how_to_apply: Step 1: Visit PM-KMY portal or nearest CSC Access the scheme via the official portal (https://maandhan.in) -> Step 2: Select 'Pradhan Mantri Kisan Maandhan Yojana' option Click Self Enrollment option (https://maandhan.in/pmkmy) -> Step 3: Enter mobile number and complete OTP verification OTP sent to...<truncated>
application_process: Step 1: Visit PM-KMY portal or nearest CSC Access the scheme via the official portal (https://maandhan.in)
Step 2: Select 'Pradhan Mantri Kisan Maandhan Yojana' option Click Self Enrollment option (https://maandhan.in/pmkmy)
Step 3: Enter mobile number and complete OTP verification OTP sent to registered mobile
Step 4: Fill enrollment form with Aadhaar, bank and land details Fill all required fields accurately
Step 5: Set up auto-debit from bank account or PM-KISAN Pension premium can be auto-deducted from PM-KISAN instalment
eligibility: Small and Marginal Farmers (land holding upto 2 hectares)
Age between 18 to 40 years at entry
Must not be covered under NPS, ESIC, EPFO
Must not be an income tax payer
Must be registered under PM-KISAN scheme (preferred but not mandatory)
conditions: Voluntary exit allowed after 10 years of contribution with interest
Contribution amount depends on age of entry (younger = lower contribution)
On exit before 60, corpus + interest returned
Premium deducted directly from PM-KISAN instalment if opted
benefits: 000 per month pension after age 60. Central Government matches farmer's contribution on 50:50 basis. Spouse (widow/widower) gets Rs. 1
500 per month on farmer's death. Nominee gets the corpus on farmer's death before 60.
sample_docs.target_beneficiaries: Small and Marginal Farmers aged 18-40 with landholding up to 2 hectares
sample_docs.financial_assistance_amount: Rs. 3,000 per month pension; Government contribution: Rs. 55 - Rs. 200 per month (matched with farmer contribution)

sample_docs.title: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana
sample_docs.summary: PMFBY provides comprehensive crop insurance from pre-sowing to post-harvest stages against natural calamities, pests and diseases. Launched in Kharif 2016, it replaced the National Agriculture Insurance Scheme (NAIS). The scheme is demand-driven and voluntary for non-loanee farmers since Kharif 2020...<truncated>
sample_docs.objective: To provide financial support to farmers suffering crop loss/damage due to unforeseen events (natural calamities, pests and diseases), stabilize farmer income and encourage modern farming practices.
sample_docs.office_addresses: Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare, Krishi Bhawan, New Delhi
sample_docs.how_to_apply: Step 1: Visit PMFBY portal Access official crop insurance portal (https://pmfby.gov.in) -> Step 2: Click on 'Farmer Application' > 'Guest Farmer' For new registration (https://pmfby.gov.in/farmerRegistrationForm) -> Step 3: Register with mobile number, Aadhaar and bank details Complete OTP verificat...<truncated>
application_process: Step 1: Visit PMFBY portal Access official crop insurance portal (https://pmfby.gov.in)
Step 2: Click on 'Farmer Application' > 'Guest Farmer' For new registration (https://pmfby.gov.in/farmerRegistrationForm)
Step 3: Register with mobile number, Aadhaar and bank details Complete OTP verification
Step 4: Select State, District, Sub-District, Village, Season, Year and Crop Enter land details and area sown
Step 5: Pay premium online (Net Banking / UPI / Debit Card) Very low premium (2% Kharif / 1.5% Rabi crops)
eligibility: All farmers (loanee and non-loanee) growing notified crops in notified areas
Loanee farmers: mandatory enrollment if crop loan availed for notified crops
Both owner and tenant farmers eligible
conditions: Must be enrolled before cut-off date (10 days from Kharif sowing / 31 days for Rabi)
Crop must be notified for the specific district/block
Post-harvest losses covered for crops left in cut and spread condition for up to 14 days
Localized risks (hailstorm, landslide, inundation) covered at individual farm level
Prevented sowing/planting covered if more than 75% area is affected
benefits: Full sum insured coverage for crop loss. Very low farmer premium (2% Kharif
5% horticulture). Balance premium paid by Government. Coverage for prevented sowing
sample_docs.coverage: Pan India - Notified crops in notified areas across all states
sample_docs.target_beneficiaries: All farmers growing notified crops in notified areas
sample_docs.financial_assistance_amount: Premium: 2% (Kharif), 1.5% (Rabi), 5% (Horticulture) of Sum Insured. Claim: Full sum insured for total loss. Government pays balance premium.
